body:
# Bug report

## Current behavior

After upgrading from `@base-ui/navigation-menu` version `1.0.0-beta.0` to `1.0.0-beta.1`, the large navigation menu is no longer scrollable. This appears to be due to the `Popover` component's `height` and `width` being set to `auto`. 

Previously, using the recommended approach from the documentation ([large menus guide](https://base-ui.com/react/components/navigation-menu#large-menus)), the `NavigationViewport` respected the constraints when the parent popup had `height` set to `--popup-height` and `max-height` set to `--available-height`. Now, those constraints seem to be ignored (width and height set to `auto`), and the `NavigationViewport` no longer properly limits its height, causing scroll to break for large content areas.

## Expected behavior

The large navigation menu should remain scrollable for overflow content.

## Reproducible example

https://stackblitz.com/edit/vitejs-vite-snc8pnss?file=src%2FApp.tsx

## Base UI version

1.0.0-beta.1

## Which browser are you using?

Chrome (latest version)

## Which OS are you using?

macOS

## Which assistive tech are you using (if applicable)?

N/A

## Additional context

**1.0.0-beta.1:**

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8143c81b-90c8-49ae-a613-3a86d0f75d65

**1.0.0-beta.0:**

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b79d26c7-36a5-4dee-ba7c-cb79829dc589

comments.body:
It's from https://github.com/mui/base-ui/pull/2070

It changes to `auto` once animations/transitions end to allow layout resizing

This feature will be available in the next npm release of Base UI.

In the meantime, you can try it out on our Canary release channel:

```sh
npm i https://pkg.pr.new/@base-ui-components/react@2242
```

title: [navigation menu] Setting `--popup-height` and `--popup-width` to `auto` breaks large menu scroll
